title: Migration of Counters
breadcrumbs: Special Considerations When Migrating from Uniface 9
text: Migration of Counters In Uniface 9, you could define runtime counters in a Define Counter dialog as if they were development object definitions that could be compiled into runtime objects. In reality, this dialog wrote the counter name and initial value into the UOBJ.TEXT table, a runtime table. In Uniface 10, counters are treated as what they are—runtime data that can only be set and read in ProcScript using numgen and numset . The counter and initial value can no longer be set in a dialog. To migrate counters from Uniface 9 to Uniface 10: If you already initialized counter data using your own logic using numgen and numset , you do not have to do anything—that will continue to work. If you used the Define Counter dialog to define and initialize counter data for the application distribution or installation process, you need to: Implement the initialization step using ProcScript instructions. Ensure that UOBJ.TEXT table is properly distributed and installed to ensure that the initial counter data is available. Related Links UOBJ.TEXT The UOBJ.TEXT entity stores runtime counters that can be addressed using the numgen and numset ProcScript statements. The associated table is usually created at runtime. numgen Increment the value of the specified counter. numset Initialize the value of the specified counter. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
